bead.title: bench-pr-A.4: per-cell loop + flock concurrency + signal handling + preflight
bead.description:
PROBLEM
With the runner skeleton (A.1), shell harness-adapters (A.2), and harbor-runner image (A.3) in place, the runner still needs the execution engine: per-cell loop, resume logic, concurrency-group flocks, signal handling, and the preflight subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
These behaviors live in cmd/bench/matrix.go's cell-execution block (process-group setsid, per-group locks, SIGINT/SIGTERM trap, resume-by-report.json). They must move to bash to complete the runner.

PROPOSED FIX
In scripts/benchmark/benchmark, implement:
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; pipe profile.json → harness-adapters/<name> → command-spec.json; compose task-spec.json; pipe → task-executors/<name> (which writes cell_dir/result.json); compose cell_dir/report.json; delete cell-state.json on terminal status.
  - Resume: skip cell when cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status. --force-rerun overrides. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock; setsid for each cell; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each in-flight cell process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight fizeau-harbor-<cell-id> container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - `preflight` subcommand: verify yq/jq/docker/setsid/flock present, fizeau-harbor-runner image buildable or pullable, required state dirs writable; exits non-zero with a checklist on failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete cmd/bench/matrix.go or harbor_adapters/*.py here — a later PR removes them.
- Do not change adapter contracts established in A.2.
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. End-to-end: `./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --reps 1 --jobs 2 --out tmp/` runs cells via shell adapter + harbor task-executor and produces report.json per cell; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd (skips when docker unavailable).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted asserts re-run with same --out skips cells with terminal report.json unless --force-rerun.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid asserts --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock asserts two cells in the same concurrency-group serialize via the documented lock path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling asserts SIGTERM stops new cells, terminates in-flight cell process groups, and issues `docker stop` for in-flight harbor containers within 30s before SIGKILL.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight asserts `./benchmark preflight` returns 0 on healthy host and non-zero with a per-check checklist when a dependency is missing.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd drives a full cell with shell adapter + harbor executor (docker-gated skip).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted verifies resume-by-report.json and --force-rerun override.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid verifies --retry-invalid semantics.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock verifies per-group flock serialization at the documented state-dir path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling verifies SIGTERM stops accepting cells, kills cell process groups, docker-stops in-flight harbor containers, escalates to SIGKILL after 30s.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight verifies preflight subcommand passes on healthy host and reports checklist failures.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
